definitions: To be swallowed or to descend into the stomach.
To be recorded or written down.
To be remembered or regarded in a particular way.
To be accepted or received in a particular way.
To decrease in amount, quality, or intensity.
To become ill with a disease.
examples: His words went down in history.
The ship went down in the storm.
Our team went down by two points.
He went down for five years.
She went down with the flu.
